name: Short Ribs of Beef Parisienne
recipeInstructions:
1. Set a large skillet over medium-high heat. When it is sizzling hot, sprinkle with 2 teaspoons salt and add the meat pieces. Brown well on all sides.
2. Season the meat with black pepper. Transfer to a bowl.
3. Add oil to the skillet and sauté onion for 2 or 3 minutes. Turn into a slow cooker.
4. Swirl water in the hot skillet and scrape up pan juices. Return to the slow cooker with the meat.
5. Cover and cook on Low for 8 to 10 hours, or until the meat is very tender.
6. Before serving, sauté mushroom slices in fat skimmed from cooking liquid over medium-high heat. Stir constantly until tender (about 5 minutes).
7. Add 1/8 teaspoon salt. Arrange short ribs on a heated serving platter and pour cooking liquid into the mushroom skillet.
8. Simmer, scraping up pan juices until sauce is as thick as heavy cream. Remove from heat.
9. Stir in sour cream and return to low heat for just long enough to warm the sauce.
10. Pour over meat and sprinkle with parsley or chervil.
